Uragoga, a plant species that belongs to the family Annonaceae, is a type of tropical evergreen tree found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and research has shown that it possesses several health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to protect against diseases such as cancer, cardiovascular disease, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Uragoga include annonaceous acetogenins, which are a group of complex compounds with potential therapeutic activity. Uragoga is often used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
